context.meal: chicken breast strips and hash brown
context.setting: Watching TV with TV on Right

episode_text:
day=4; meal=chicken breast strips and hash brown; setting=Watching TV with TV on Right; time_of_day=evening;
preferences: microwave_time=3 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize right occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=1000 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food is cold-served fruit or dessert AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is personal or social THEN no microwave BECAUSE the observed cold-food meal used no reheating, consistent with intended cold serving. IF food is a warm savory meal AND affective state is neutral AND dining context involves working on a laptop or watching TV THEN 3 min BECAUSE repeated warm savory meals in attention-directed contexts were reheated for 3 min, indicating a stable preference for thoroughly warming hot foods in those settings.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a salient person or focal activity in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user selected front-occlusion minimization in a front-social context and repeated laptop-working contexts, showing concern about preserving the forward view when attention is directed forward. IF dining context includes a focal activity to the right of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the TV-on-right meal used right-occlusion minimization, indicating the user wants the robot to avoid blocking the attended side.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social or involves a concurrent visually attended activity AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN slow BECAUSE the user selected slow speed across all observed meals, suggesting a stable comfort-first interaction style rather than prioritizing speed. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food requires fork acquisition AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is laptop work or previously observed general contexts THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected this option, favoring reliable bite acquisition. IF food is a warm savory item in strips or pieces AND affective state is neutral AND dining context involves watching TV to the right THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the chicken-strip meal used parallel skewering, suggesting the user may sometimes prefer narrower bites for certain solid foods.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social or involves a concurrent task such as laptop work AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food requiring bite acquisition THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected explicit web confirmation in earlier observed meals, indicating a preference to verify bite acquisition before transfer when errors may be more disruptive. IF dining context involves watching TV to the right AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is a warm savory meal requiring bite acquisition THEN no BECAUSE the user skipped confirmation in that TV-viewing meal, suggesting a willingness to reduce interaction burden when attention is directed to entertainment rather than social coordination.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF dining context is any observed context AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to limited trunk control, and all observed meals used inside-mouth transfer. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE outside-mouth distance does not apply, and the user has consistently selected inside-mouth transfer. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social, involves working on a laptop, or involves watching TV AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any task THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED in the social meal, the TV-viewing meal, and later laptop meals, indicating a stable preference for quiet nonverbal initiation cues. A single earlier laptop observation used speech + LED, but repeated evidence favors LED as the user's general signaling style.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is feeding THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and previously selected open-mouth readiness in a social meal, indicating low-effort initiation can outweigh social ambiguity concerns. IF dining context involves working on a laptop or watching TV AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is feeding THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected autocontinue in repeated attention-divided contexts, showing a stable tendency to minimize interaction burden when focused on another activity.

IF dining context is social AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is drinking THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and previously selected open-mouth readiness for drink initiation. IF dining context involves working on a laptop or watching TV AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is drinking THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected autocontinue in repeated attention-divided contexts, again preferring lower-effort automation when occupied.

IF dining context is social AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is mouth wiping THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and previously selected open-mouth readiness for wiping initiation. IF dining context involves working on a laptop or watching TV AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is mouth wiping THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected autocontinue in repeated attention-divided contexts, consistent with preferring reduced interaction demand when occupied.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social, involves working on a laptop, or involves watching TV AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any task THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected nonverbal completion cues across these observed contexts, indicating a stable preference for quiet visual signaling rather than speech.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF task is feeding AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context THEN perception BECAUSE the user selected sensor-based bite completion detection in all observed meals, and this avoids button presses the user cannot reliably perform. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF task is drinking AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons or perform head gestures, and all observed meals used autocontinue. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF task is mouth wiping AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons or perform head gestures, and all observed meals used autocontinue. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a salient person or focal activity in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected retracting between bites in a front-social context and repeated laptop-working contexts, indicating willingness to accept longer meal time to reduce prolonged visual obstruction when the forward view matters. IF dining context includes a focal activity to the right of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN yes BECAUSE the TV-on-right meal also used retracting, extending the same preference to side-oriented visual attention.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food has no dip or sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not applicable. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social AND affective state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the observed social meal with whipped cream used less dipping, consistent with limiting messiness. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves working on a laptop AND affective state is neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed laptop meal with ranch dressing used no dipping, suggesting a stronger mess-avoidance preference when multitasking around equipment.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any task using autocontinue THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user selected a moderate wait in the observed social meal, balancing time to respond with meal efficiency. IF dining context involves working on a laptop or watching TV AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any task using autocontinue THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected the longest wait in repeated attention-divided contexts, indicating a strong preference for extra time before automatic progression when attention is shared with another activity.
